notFound.heading: Article not found
notFound.body: We couldn't find the article you're looking for. It may have been moved or removed.

genericBody: This article is part of the New York State knowledge base. Detailed step-by-step guidance is being added.
For personalized help, contact the Enterprise Service Desk or start a guided recovery from the home page.

data-privacy.text: New York State is committed to protecting the personal information of every resident. This article explains what data we collect, how it is used, and the steps you can take to keep your information safe.
State services collect only the information needed to deliver them. That typically includes your contact details, account identifiers, and records of the requests you make.

items: Contact details such as your name and email address
Account identifiers used to verify your identity
Technical data such as browser type and pages visited

data-privacy.text: Use a strong, unique password, enable multi-factor authentication, and be cautious about the information you share online. Review your account settings regularly.

webex.text: Webex is the state's video conferencing platform for meetings, webinars, and screen sharing. You can join from a browser, the desktop app, or your phone.
Select the meeting link in your invitation. If prompted, allow access to your camera and microphone, then choose whether to join with video on or off.

items: Test your audio and video before joining
Mute yourself when you are not speaking
Use the chat panel to share links and questions

webex.text: Select Share Content during a meeting, choose the window or screen you want to present, and select Share. Stop sharing when you are finished.

smartphone-safety.text: Your smartphone holds a lot of personal information. A few settings and habits go a long way toward keeping it — and your accounts — secure.

items: Set a strong passcode and enable biometric unlock
Enable remote find-and-erase in case your phone is lost

smartphone-safety.text: Install apps only from official stores, review the permissions each app requests, and remove apps you no longer use.

microsoft-outlook.text: Microsoft Outlook brings your email, calendar, and contacts together in one place. This guide covers the basics to get you organized.
Use folders and rules to sort incoming mail automatically, and flag messages that need follow-up so nothing slips through.

items: Create folders for projects and topics
Set up rules to file mail automatically
Use search folders to find messages fast

microsoft-outlook.text: Schedule meetings, set reminders, and share your availability. Color-categorize events to see your day at a glance.
